title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear a woman talking to her son about a school bag.
extracts.audio_script: Mother: Have you packed your school bag for tomorrow? || Boy: Nearly. I've got my books and sports kit. || Mother: Don't forget your science project. || Boy: [8]I left it by the front door so I wouldn't forget it in the morning[/8]. I still need to put my calculator in the side pocket, though. It should be fine as long as everybody remembers the new arrangement. I am glad we checked the details before making the final decision.

dialogue.speaker: mother
dialogue.text: Have you packed your school bag for tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Nearly. I've got my books and sports kit.

dialogue.speaker: mother
dialogue.text: Don't forget your science project.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: I left it by the front door so I wouldn't forget it in the morning. I still need to put my calculator in the side pocket, though. It should be fine as long as everybody remembers the new arrangement. I am glad we checked the details before making the final decision.

questions.question: Where is the boy's science project?
questions.explanation: The boy says he left the science project by the front door.

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a pet.
extracts.audio_script: Girl: How's your new dog settling in? || Boy: Pretty well. He sleeps a lot during the day, but he's active in the evening. || Girl: Has he chewed anything yet? || Boy: Only one shoe. [9]The main problem is that he barks whenever the doorbell rings[/9]. We're trying to teach him to stay calm. That gives us enough time to prepare without changing everything else. It also means we will not have to spend extra money unnecessarily.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: How's your new dog settling in?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Pretty well. He sleeps a lot during the day, but he's active in the evening.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Has he chewed anything yet?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Only one shoe. The main problem is that he barks whenever the doorbell rings. We're trying to teach him to stay calm. That gives us enough time to prepare without changing everything else. It also means we will not have to spend extra money unnecessarily.

questions.question: What problem does the boy mention?

options.B: The dog barks at the doorbell.

questions.explanation: The boy says the main problem is that the dog barks whenever the doorbell rings.

extracts.context: You hear a teacher talking about a class project.
extracts.audio_script: Teacher: For your geography project, remember that you can work alone or in pairs. || Teacher: Some students asked if posters are allowed. They are, but they must include your own writing. || Teacher: [10]The most important thing is to compare two different places, not just describe one town[/10]. Please hand in your plan by Friday. I suppose it is a small change, but it makes the situation much easier. That should make the plan easier and help everyone know what to expect.

dialogue.text: For your geography project, remember that you can work alone or in pairs.

questions.question: What must students do in their project?
questions.explanation: The teacher says the most important thing is to compare two different places.

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a swimming race.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Well done in the swimming race yesterday. Did you win? || Man: No, I came second, but I was happy with that. || Woman: Why? || Man: [11]I swam faster than my previous best time[/11], and that's what I wanted. The winner trains every day, so I didn't expect to beat him. I think that is the most sensible choice after looking at the other possibilities. It means there should be fewer problems when the time comes to do it.

dialogue.text: Well done in the swimming race yesterday. Did you win?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I swam faster than my previous best time, and that's what I wanted. The winner trains every day, so I didn't expect to beat him. I think that is the most sensible choice after looking at the other possibilities. It means there should be fewer problems when the time comes to do it.

questions.question: Why was the man happy with the race?
questions.explanation: The man says he swam faster than his previous best time.

extracts.context: You hear a woman talking about a cooking app.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you still using that cooking app? || Woman: Yes, but mainly for shopping now. || Man: I thought it gave you recipes. || Woman: It does, but [12]I like it because it makes a list of everything I need to buy[/12]. That stops me forgetting things when I'm at the supermarket. I would rather decide now than leave everything until the last minute. That way, we can enjoy it more and avoid rushing around later.

questions.explanation: The woman likes the app because it makes a list of everything she needs to buy.

extracts.context: You hear two people talking about a birthday meal.
extracts.audio_script: Boy: Where are you having your birthday meal this year? The pizza place again? || Girl: No, it was too noisy last time. || Boy: What about the Chinese restaurant? || Girl: [13]We're going to the café by the river because it has outdoor tables[/13]. If the weather stays warm, it'll be nicer than sitting inside. It is not perfect, but it seems better than the other choices we had. The important thing is to keep it simple and not make extra work.

questions.question: Where will the girl have her birthday meal?

options.C: at a café by the river

questions.explanation: The girl says they are going to the café by the river.
